title: Karva Chauth Vrat Katha
intro: The sacred story of Karva Chauth, an auspicious fast observed by married women for the longevity and well-being of their husbands.
description:
Karva Chauth is a traditional fast primarily observed by married women, particularly in Northern India. The fast involves not eating or drinking from sunrise to moonrise. The vrat katha (story) is an integral part of this sacred tradition.

|| The Story ||
Once, there was a woman who was deeply devoted to Lord Shiva. She observed the Karva Chauth fast with pure heart and devotion. Lord Shiva blessed her with prosperity and her husband's long life and happiness.

The significance of Karva Chauth lies in the deep bond of love and devotion between husband and wife.
meaning: Karva Chauth celebrates the sacred bond between spouses and the woman's dedication to her husband's well-being and longevity. It is observed on the fourth day after the full moon.

faq.a1: Primarily married women, though unmarried women may also observe it hoping for a good husband.
faq.q2: What is the significance of the vrat katha?
faq.a2: The story carries spiritual significance and strengthens the faith and devotion of the person fasting.
faq.a3: On the fourth lunar day after the full moon, typically in October-November.
